---
title: "Making New Friends in Recovery"
format: html
---

# Making New Friends in Recovery

Social environment is one of the strongest predictors of recovery outcomes. Building new, supportive relationships can reduce isolation, normalize sober living, and increase accountability. This takes time and often feels uncomfortable at first.

A helpful approach is to focus on **shared activities** rather than immediate closeness. Regular contact around healthy routines often becomes friendship naturally.

Social network change is a high-impact recovery intervention. Increasing contact with pro-recovery peers reduces exposure to drinking norms and strengthens support during vulnerable moments.

## Take a moment to answer the prompts below:

### Who do you currently spend the most time with?

| Person | Supports recovery? (Y/N) | How I feel after spending time with them |
|---------------|---------------------|------------------------------------|
|        |                          |                                          |
|        |                          |                                          |
|        |                          |                                          |

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### What feels missing right now?

-   [ ] People who don’t drink\
-   [ ] People who understand recovery\
-   [ ] People to do activities with\
-   [ ] Emotional support\
-   [ ] Accountability\
-   [ ] Other: \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

## What makes making new friends difficult right now?

-   [ ] Social anxiety\
-   [ ] Not knowing where to go\
-   [ ] Fear of rejection\
-   [ ] Low energy / motivation\
-   [ ] Trust concerns\
-   [ ] Other: \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

::: callout
**Complete these:**

-   “Making friends in recovery might feel uncomfortable because…”

-   “That discomfort does NOT mean…”\
:::

**Remember: Friendships rarely form instantly. Progress looks like showing up again, recognizing familiar faces, short conversations, and gradual comfort.**

![](https://github.com/jjcurtin/aud_support/blob/main/modules/images/bench.png?raw=true){fig-align="center"}
